journeys.title: Course Planning & Enrolment Journey
journeys.description: Journey map focused purely on the course planning and enrolment experience for current students.

personas.title: Sam — Planning their path
personas.bio: Needs to figure out subjects, prerequisites, and enrolment across hub and faculty

steps.title: Get started & accept
steps.description: Doing: Accept offer, enrol for the first time; Look for orientation and setup steps | Feeling: Where do I begin?
steps.channels: Hub enrolment steps; Faculty orientation pages; Law/FEIT offer-acceptance steps; my.unimelb; students.unimelb.edu.au; Faculty sites

steps.title: Plan my course
steps.description: Doing: Map out my degree; Find sample plans; Understand major requirements | Feeling: Scattered plans
steps.channels: Hub: My Course Planner + Handbook; Faculty: sample plans, major guides; My Course Planner; Faculty subdomains (MBS, Biomedical); handbook.unimelb.edu.au

steps.title: Enrol & choose subjects
steps.description: Doing: Enrol in subjects; Check quota subjects; Apply for prerequisite waivers | Feeling: Wait, which rules?
steps.channels: Hub enrolment pages; Faculty cohort-specific subject rules (e.g. Law JD); my.unimelb; students.unimelb.edu.au/course-admin

steps.title: Manage timetable & load
steps.description: Doing: Build timetable; Overload or cross-institutional study | Feeling: Getting sorted
steps.channels: MyTimetable; Hub study load pages; Arts links directly to hub (good example); MyTimetable; hub course-admin

insights.title: Faculty offer-acceptance steps sometimes duplicate the central process
insights.description: Some faculties like Law and FEIT have their own offer-acceptance/orientation steps which overlap with central enrolment.

insights.title: Orientation content is mixed with administrative enrolment steps
insights.description: Orientation isn't just about events; faculties embed transactional enrolment mechanics into their orientation flows.

insights.title: Planning split across hub tool and faculty sample plans
insights.description: The hub holds the planning tools (My Course Planner, Handbook) while faculties hold the degree-specific sample plans — and the two don't link cleanly, so students bounce between them.

insights.title: FEIT re-hosts My Course Planner
insights.description: FEIT's top two course-planning pages are 'My Course Planner' and 'Faculty Course Planning Resources' — literally re-hosting the central tool to keep in sync.

insights.title: 46% of course planning is on school subdomains (MBS, Biomedical)
insights.description: Of 207 course-planning pages, 46% live on two school subdomains (Melbourne Business School and Biomedical Sciences) beneath their faculties — a level deeper than the faculty site.

insights.title: Faculties restate hub enrolment mechanics
insights.description: The hub owns the transactional act of enrolment, but faculties restate generic 'how to enrol/re-enrol' steps unnecessarily.

insights.title: Generic 'how to enrol' restatements add confusion
insights.description: When a faculty restates the central process, students don't know if the faculty page or the hub page is the definitive source of truth.

insights.title: Multiple explanations of the same process
insights.description: Enrolment steps explained differently on different sites.

insights.title: Faculty pages restate census dates and overloading rules
insights.description: Faculties re-explain rules for overloading and census dates, instead of pointing to the definitive policy.

insights.title: Timetabling explained multiple ways
insights.description: Different faculties write their own guides to MyTimetable, creating a maintenance burden and potential inconsistency.

opportunities.title: How might we give every new student one consistent path to accept and enrol?
opportunities.description: A single, clear flow to accept offers and complete core administrative enrolment centrally.

opportunities.title: How might we let a student plan their whole degree without hopping between hub and faculty?
opportunities.description: A hub course-planning gateway linking every faculty's sample plans — with faculties linking the planner, not re-hosting it — lets a student plan in one flow.

opportunities.title: How might we make sure enrolment is explained once, in one place?
opportunities.description: Declare the hub the single source of truth for the transactional spine: enrolment mechanics. Faculties link, never restate.

opportunities.title: How might we ensure students see the definitive hub rules for load and timetabling?
opportunities.description: Each faculty should expose one consistent 'Enrolment, results & admin' landing page whose entire job is to link to the hub's transactional pages and surface the faculty's genuine local exceptions.

solutions.title: Centralised offer acceptance and enrolment path
Faculties only handle discipline-specific orientation events
Faculties link to planner instead of re-hosting
Hub is the single source of truth for enrolment mechanics
Keep only genuine local exceptions (forms, prerequisite waivers)
One consistent 'Enrolment, results & admin' landing per faculty pointing to hub
